originalTitle: Suspected suicide attacks kill at least 23 in north-east Nigeria
title: A Sad Day in Maiduguri, Nigeria, and How People Are Helping
content:
Hello, my little explorers and curious minds! It's your friend here, bringing you news from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we have some important news from a place called Nigeria.

What Happened in Maiduguri?

Sometimes, sad things happen. It's okay to feel a little bit sad when we hear about them. This week, on a Monday evening, some loud noises happened. These loud noises were in a city called Maiduguri. Maiduguri is in a country named Nigeria. Nigeria is a very big and beautiful country. It is in West Africa. It has many different people. And amazing cultures! These loud noises were not good. They made many people feel scared. Many people were just going about their day. They were in busy places. Like markets. Or streets where many people walk and talk. Did you know? Nigeria is home to over 200 million people! That's like having more than 200 million friends and families!

When People Get Hurt

When the loud noises happened, some people got hurt. It's very sad when anyone gets hurt. Our news tells us that about 23 people got hurt badly. Some even lost their lives. That means they are no longer with us. This makes many people in Maiduguri very, very sad. And all over Nigeria too. We send them big hugs. And our warmest wishes. When sad things like this happen, it's like a dark cloud. But remember, after every dark cloud, the sun always tries to shine again. Did you know? When someone is hurt, doctors and nurses are like superheroes! They work so hard to make people feel better and strong again.

Who Helps When Things Are Difficult?

When these loud noises happened, many good people came to help. Police officers came. Firefighters came. Kind neighbours quickly rushed to the places. They helped people who were hurt. They took them to hospitals. Hospitals are special places. Doctors and nurses take care of people there. These helpers are brave. They are very kind. They want to make sure everyone is safe. They are like good shepherds. They look after their flock. They try to keep everyone out of harm's way. Did you know? In many African cultures, people believe in 'Ubuntu'. It means "I am because we are." It's all about helping each other and being kind.

Why Did This Happen?

This is a tricky part to explain, my friends. Sometimes, a few people do very, very wrong things. These people use things like loud bombs. These bombs make noises and hurt others. This is not good. And it is not right. We don't know exactly *why* they do these hurtful things. But we know it causes a lot of pain. Grown-ups, like the police. And government leaders. They are working very hard to find these people. They want to make sure that Maiduguri is a safe place. And all of Nigeria. Especially for children like you. Did you know? Governments have special people called peacekeepers. They work to stop bad things from happening. They want to bring people together.

How People Come Together

Even though something sad happened, people in Maiduguri are very strong. They are helping each other. Neighbours are checking on neighbours. Friends are comforting friends. People are sharing food. And water. They are praying for those who are hurt. And for the families who lost loved ones. This shows us how kind people can be. Even when things are very hard. It reminds us that love and kindness are stronger. Stronger than any sadness. Or any hurt. Did you know? Many communities in Africa have special ways to support each other. They sing songs. They tell stories. And they share meals together.

Keeping Safe and Hopeful

It's important for you, my little friends, to know this: Grown-ups are always working to keep you safe. Your parents. Your teachers. Your community leaders. They all care about you very much. If you ever hear something that makes you scared. Or confused. It's always good to talk to a grown-up you trust. They can explain things to you. And help you understand. We hope that peace will come back to Maiduguri very soon. And we will keep sending our love. And good thoughts. To all the people there. Did you know? Talking about your feelings, whether happy or sad, helps you feel better. It's okay to feel sad or confused sometimes.

A Message for You

Remember to always be kind to each other. A small act of kindness can make a big difference. Let's also think about all the good things happening in Africa. There are so many amazing stories! Stories of friendship. Of discovery. And of joy! We will keep sharing those stories with you. Right here. Keep shining brightly, little stars of Africa! Did you know? Nigeria is famous for its lively music! And colourful movies. They call them "Nollywood"! It's a land full of creativity.
summary: Sad things happened in Maiduguri, Nigeria, but many kind people are helping those who got hurt, and grown-ups are working to keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: It matters because when sad things happen, we learn about helping each other and staying strong as a community, and grown-ups work hard to make sure our homes are safe places.

originalTitle: 'Fire came from the sky and burned them' - life on the brink of civil war in South Sudan
title: 'Fire came from the sky and burned them' - life on the brink of civil war in South Sudan
content: Some 50,000 people are told to flee their homes as the army warns of a huge military offensive in Jonglei state.

originalTitle: Russia agrees to stop using Kenyan recruits in Ukraine conflict, Kenya says
title: Good News for Our Friends in Kenya!
content:
Hello everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some very important and happy news from far, far away. We're talking about a country called Kenya, right here in Africa, and a country called Russia, which is very far from us. Grab your juice boxes and get ready to listen!

Big News! No More Kenyans for Far-Away Fights!

Guess what? There's a big, happy update! The country of Russia has said they will stop asking people from Kenya to go and fight in a far-away place called Ukraine. This is super good news, because it means our friends from Kenya will be safer and won't be tricked anymore. It's like when your parents say, 'No more sweets before dinner!' – it's for your own good, right?

What Was Happening?

For a little while, some grown-ups from Kenya decided to travel very far from their homes. They went all the way to a place called Ukraine. It's so far that it would take many, many hours on an airplane to get there! We heard that about 1,000 Kenyans went to this far-away place. That's like a whole lot of classrooms full of people!

Why Did They Go So Far?

This is where it gets a little tricky. Many of these Kenyans say they were 'lured under false pretences.' What does that mean? It means they were tricked! Imagine someone tells you, 'Come play with us, we have a giant cake!' But when you get there, there's no cake, and instead, they ask you to do something hard or scary. That's a bit like what happened. People were promised good jobs or nice things, but really, they were asked to join a big, serious fight.

What is a 'Fight' Like That?

When we talk about a 'fight' in the news, we don't mean a small argument with your brother or sister. We mean a really big, serious problem between countries. It's called a 'conflict.' In Ukraine, there has been a big conflict, which means people are sadly hurting each other. It's a very dangerous place to be, and certainly not a good place for anyone, especially if they didn't know they were going there to fight.

Did You Know?

Did you know that Kenya is a beautiful country in East Africa? It's famous for its amazing wildlife, like lions and elephants, and vast plains! Ukraine, on the other hand, is a country in Europe, a whole different continent, very far away from Africa! It's important to know where places are, so you understand how far these Kenyans traveled to a totally different part of the world.

Kenya's Government Stepped In!

Our grown-ups, the leaders of Kenya – we call them the 'government' – heard about their people being in Ukraine and being tricked. They were worried! So, the Kenyan government talked to the government of Russia. They said, 'Please, stop asking our people to join these fights, especially if they are being tricked.' Our government wants to keep all Kenyans safe, no matter where they are. They are like very big parents looking out for their children.

Russia Said 'Okay!'

And guess what? Russia listened! They agreed to stop using Kenyans in the conflict in Ukraine. This is wonderful news because it means fewer people will be tricked, and more Kenyans can stay safe at home or wherever they choose to be, without the danger of fighting. An 'agreement' means two groups shook hands and said they would do something together, or in this case, one group promised to stop doing something.

Why Is This Important?

It's very important because everyone deserves to be safe and to know the truth. When someone tricks you, it's not fair. This news means that Kenya's government cares a lot about its people, and they worked hard to make sure their citizens are protected. It also means that other countries listened to Kenya, which is a big deal! It shows that talking nicely can solve big problems.

More About Being Tricked (False Pretences)

Let's talk a little more about 'false pretences.' Imagine someone says, 'Come with me, I have a cool new video game for you!' But when you go, there's no game, and they want you to clean their room instead. That's a 'false pretence.' It's not telling the truth. It's always good to ask questions and make sure you know what you're really going to do before you go somewhere far away with someone, especially if it sounds too good to be true.

Did You Know? (Again!)

Did you know that governments around the world talk to each other all the time? It's called 'diplomacy'! It's how they solve problems and make agreements, just like Kenya and Russia did. It's like when you and your friend talk out a disagreement instead of just being mad – talking helps everyone understand and get along much better!

Keeping Our Friends Safe

This whole story is about keeping people safe. It's about making sure that no one is tricked into doing dangerous things, especially not far from home. We're very happy that Kenya's government worked hard for its people, and that Russia listened to their worries. This shows that every country cares about its people. So, remember, always listen to your parents and teachers, and be careful who you trust, especially if they promise you something that sounds super amazing but also a little bit strange or too good to be true! That's all for today's good news from your friendly reporter. Stay safe and keep smiling!
summary: Russia has agreed to stop taking people from Kenya to fight in Ukraine, after Kenya's government asked them to, helping keep Kenyans safe from being tricked.
whyMatters: This is important because it means people from Kenya will not be tricked into going to a dangerous place to fight anymore, and their government is working hard to keep them safe at home and around the world.
